# Test Review: `lib/features/ai/utils/` and `test/features/ai/utils/`

**Scope:** `lib/features/ai/utils/` (the small `utils/` directory, distinct from `util/`) and `test/features/ai/utils/`.

*Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis)*

---

## File inventory

| File | Lines | Has test? | Top issue |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lib/features/ai/utils/checklist_validation.dart` | 78 | Yes (`checklist_validation_test.dart`, 515 lines) | Test file is 6.6× the impl size; some explicit-case tests duplicate what the Glados property already covers |
| `test/features/ai/utils/checklist_validation_test.dart` | 515 | — | Oversized relative to impl; Glados present and well-formed; some static tests are redundant with the property |

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/features/ai/utils/checklist_validation_test.dart` — 515 lines for 78 lines of impl. This ratio (6.6:1) is high. The file covers three groups: `validateItems`, `validateItemEntry`, and `isValidBatchSize`/`getBatchSizeErrorMessage`. The Glados property (`numRuns: 180`) already covers the full cross-product of entry shapes via `_GeneratedChecklistValidationScenario`. Several of the static `validateItems` test cases (lines 137–210) duplicate shapes that the generator also covers. Removing the duplicate static cases would bring the file to ~300 lines without losing coverage. No split needed; simplification is the right action.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [ ] **[MED]** `checklist_validation_test.dart:170–190` — the `'should handle various isChecked values'` test constructs 6 items (true, false, null, 'true', 1, 0) and asserts the `isChecked` outcome for each. The generator already has a `validCheckedString` shape (which represents `isChecked: 'true'` → false) and a `validChecked` shape (which represents `isChecked: true` → true). At minimum, the `null`, `1`, and `0` cases could be collapsed into a note in the generator rather than a separate static test, as the Glados run at `numRuns: 180` will cover them. However, keeping explicit static cases for `null` and non-boolean coercion is defensible as worked examples — see the convention in `test/README.md`. Flag as informational.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `checklist_validation_test.dart` — the generator class `_GeneratedChecklistValidationScenario` and its extensions are 122 lines (lines 4–122), accounting for nearly a quarter of the file. This is appropriate for the complexity of the generator. No issue.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `checklist_validation_test.dart:374–395` — `isValidBatchSize` tests use the pattern `expect(ChecklistValidation.isValidBatchSize(N), true/false)` with explicit boolean literals. Prefer `isTrue`/`isFalse` matchers for consistency with the rest of the test suite.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [ ] **[MED]** The existing Glados test at lines 261–292 covers `validateItems` and `validateItemEntry` together via `_GeneratedChecklistValidationScenario`. It is tagged correctly with `tags: 'glados'` and uses `numRuns: 180`. This is well-done. Consider adding a second Glados property specifically for `isValidBatchSize` and `getBatchSizeErrorMessage`:
  - Property: for any `size` in `[1, maxBatchSize]`, `isValidBatchSize(size) == true`.
  - Property: for any `size` outside that range, `isValidBatchSize(size) == false`.
  - Property: `getBatchSizeErrorMessage(0)` contains `'No valid items found'`; `getBatchSizeErrorMessage(size > maxBatchSize)` contains `'Too many items'`.
  These could be expressed as a `Glados(any.intInRange(1, ChecklistValidation.maxBatchSize))` test, which is simple and fast.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `ChecklistValidation.maxTitleLength` (400) is a constant that feeds the `title.length <= maxTitleLength` boundary in `validateItems` and `trimmedTitle.length > maxTitleLength` in `validateItemEntry`. The generator already produces a `tooLongTitle` shape (`'x' * 401`). A Glados property using `any.intInRange(1, 400)` to generate valid title lengths and `any.intInRange(401, 1000)` for invalid ones would be robust. The current static cases only test 400 exactly (not covered) and 401 (covered). Add at least a `title.length == 400` exact-boundary static case.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [ ] **[MED]** `ChecklistValidation.getBatchSizeErrorMessage` — the `actualSize == 0` branch (line 71, returns `'No valid items found...'`) and the `actualSize > maxBatchSize` branch (line 73, returns `'Too many items...'`) have static tests (lines 396–415), but the fallback branch (`'Invalid batch size: $actualSize'` for negative values, lines 76–77) is never exercised. Add a test for `getBatchSizeErrorMessage(-1)` or similar to cover the last branch.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `ChecklistValidation.maxTitleLength` exactly-at-boundary (a title of exactly 400 characters) is never tested. Add `expect(ChecklistValidation.validateItems([{'title': 'x' * 400}]), hasLength(1))`.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `validateItemEntry` with a `null` entry (not a `Map`, not a `String`) — the test at line 314 uses `123` (int). A `null` entry would hit the `is! Map<String, dynamic>` branch and return the generic error. Add a test for `validateItemEntry(null)`.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [ ] **[LOW]** `checklist_validation_test.dart` — all tests are synchronous pure-function calls. No I/O, no timers, no widget pumps. The Glados run (`numRuns: 180`) is the main CPU cost, which is expected and correctly routed to the Glados CI lane via `tags: 'glados'`.
- [ ] **[LOW]** No fake-time violations or `DateTime.now()` usages found.

---

## Summary

- **Well-covered impl** overall; `ChecklistValidation` is the most thoroughly tested class in the target scope.
- **1 minor coverage gap**: `getBatchSizeErrorMessage` fallback branch and exact title-length boundary (400 chars) are untested.
- **1 redundancy**: some static `validateItems` test cases overlap with what the Glados generator already covers; removing them would shrink the file by ~60 lines.
- **Glados is present, correctly tagged, and well-formed** — the existing property is a strong example to follow.
- **No speed issues**, no fake-time violations.
